1.header: Messages Can Be Fake Even If They Look Official

1.body: Scam messages often copy real company logos, names, and formatting. They may look like they came from your bank, a delivery service, or a government office. Visual polish does not guarantee legitimacy.

1.header: Unexpected Messages Are A Warning Sign

1.body: Be cautious when you receive messages you were not expecting — especially about account problems, prizes, or urgent requests. Scammers rely on surprise to lower your guard.

1.header: Urgency Is a Common Scam Tactic

1.body: Phrases like “act now,” “account will be closed,” or “payment required today” are meant to pressure you. Real companies rarely demand instant action by message.

1.takeaway-body: If a message creates urgency or asks for sensitive information, slow down and verify before taking any action.
